splitDefinition.method: Stable Fisher-Yates shuffle over scenario indices using Mulberry32 seed 0xc0def00d.

evaluationDesign.causalHistory: referencePoll rows with elapsedMicroseconds <= anchor elapsedMicroseconds
evaluationDesign.label: interpolated referencePoll at anchor + horizon

features.productEligibilityNote: Scheduler delay is causal only if the runtime exposes the latest scheduler timing sample at anchor time.

modelList.description: Step 2 baseline/teacher: LS velocity over 12 causal referencePoll samples, cap64.
Validation-selected causal speed/stillness/gap gate switching constant, LS8, and LS12.
Global ridge residual correction over causal features.
Horizon-segmented ridge residual correction over the same causal features.
Dependency-free piecewise residual mean by horizon, causal speed bin, and scheduler-delay bin.
Non-product oracle: uses script-derived hold/resume/moving category to switch baselines.

bestModelPerSegment.rankingMetric: lowest p95 error, then lowest mean error

fsmnFamilyPlan.reason: Start with the smallest temporal memory over causal deltas and LS residuals.

fsmnFamilyPlan.firstSize: shared causal memory plus scheduler-delay and horizon conditioning
fsmnFamilyPlan.reason: Useful if scheduler-delay bins explain stress tails.

fsmnFamilyPlan.firstSize: velocity-focused input branch with LS12/LS8/last2 projections
fsmnFamilyPlan.reason: Resume and moving segments are velocity/residual dominated.
Only escalate here if Step 3 residual features improve p95 without tail regressions.
